strap: Greater Glasgow Fintech draws £614m of UKRI lead-led funding across 3,938 grants, anchored by Glasgow (49%), Strathclyde (36%). 19 Companies House-traced spin-outs translate to £32m UKRI per spin-out.
behaviour: The cluster shows low-confidence "Re-proving instead of narrowing" behaviour — research narrative is reinforced by recurring programme launches rather than narrowing toward commercial scaling, with academic capacity reabsorbing the cluster's signal. Pipeline 1 evidence: the £614m UKRI footprint is overwhelmingly academic — 85% University of Glasgow + Strathclyde combined — across multiple accelerator/incubator programmes and university-support partnerships rather than narrowing toward specific FinTech sub-domains.
